src/backend/utils/misc/guc_tables.c
1/*--------------------------------------------------------------------
2 *
3 * guc_tables.c
4 *
5 * Static tables for the Grand Unified Configuration scheme.
6 *
7 * Many of these tables are const.  However, ConfigureNamesBool[]
8 * and so on are not, because the structs in those arrays are actually
9 * the live per-variable state data that guc.c manipulates.  While many of
10 * their fields are intended to be constant, some fields change at runtime.
11 *
12 *
13 * Copyright (c) 2000-2025, PostgreSQL Global Development Group
14 * Written by Peter Eisentraut <peter_e@gmx.net>.
15 *
16 * IDENTIFICATION
17 *	  src/backend/utils/misc/guc_tables.c
18 *
19 *--------------------------------------------------------------------
20 */
21#include "postgres.h"
22
23#ifdef HAVE_COPYFILE_H
24#include <copyfile.h>
25#endif
26#include <float.h>
27#include <limits.h>
28#ifdef HAVE_SYSLOG
29#include <syslog.h>
30#endif
31
32#include "access/commit_ts.h"

56#include "libpq/oauth.h"

110/* This value is normally passed in from the Makefile */
111#ifndef PG_KRB_SRVTAB
112#define PG_KRB_SRVTAB ""
113#endif
114
115/*
116 * Options for enum values defined in this module.
117 *
118 * NOTE! Option values may not contain double quotes!
119 */
120
121static const struct config_enum_entry bytea_output_options[] = {
122	{"escape", BYTEA_OUTPUT_ESCAPE, false},
123	{"hex", BYTEA_OUTPUT_HEX, false},
124	{NULL, 0, false}
125};
126
127StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(bytea_output_options) == (BYTEA_OUTPUT_HEX + 2),
128				 "array length mismatch");
129
130/*
131 * We have different sets for client and server message level options because
132 * they sort slightly different (see "log" level), and because "fatal"/"panic"
133 * aren't sensible for client_min_messages.
134 */
135static const struct config_enum_entry client_message_level_options[] = {
136	{"debug5", DEBUG5, false},
137	{"debug4", DEBUG4, false},
138	{"debug3", DEBUG3, false},
139	{"debug2", DEBUG2, false},
140	{"debug1", DEBUG1, false},
141	{"debug", DEBUG2, true},
142	{"log", LOG, false},
143	{"info", INFO, true},
144	{"notice", NOTICE, false},
145	{"warning", WARNING, false},
146	{"error", ERROR, false},
147	{NULL, 0, false}
148};
149
150static const struct config_enum_entry server_message_level_options[] = {
151	{"debug5", DEBUG5, false},
152	{"debug4", DEBUG4, false},
153	{"debug3", DEBUG3, false},
154	{"debug2", DEBUG2, false},
155	{"debug1", DEBUG1, false},
156	{"debug", DEBUG2, true},
157	{"info", INFO, false},
158	{"notice", NOTICE, false},
159	{"warning", WARNING, false},
160	{"error", ERROR, false},
161	{"log", LOG, false},
162	{"fatal", FATAL, false},
163	{"panic", PANIC, false},
164	{NULL, 0, false}
165};
166
167static const struct config_enum_entry intervalstyle_options[] = {
168	{"postgres", INTSTYLE_POSTGRES, false},
169	{"postgres_verbose", INTSTYLE_POSTGRES_VERBOSE, false},
170	{"sql_standard", INTSTYLE_SQL_STANDARD, false},
171	{"iso_8601", INTSTYLE_ISO_8601, false},
172	{NULL, 0, false}
173};
174
175static const struct config_enum_entry icu_validation_level_options[] = {
176	{"disabled", -1, false},
177	{"debug5", DEBUG5, false},
178	{"debug4", DEBUG4, false},
179	{"debug3", DEBUG3, false},
180	{"debug2", DEBUG2, false},
181	{"debug1", DEBUG1, false},
182	{"debug", DEBUG2, true},
183	{"log", LOG, false},
184	{"info", INFO, true},
185	{"notice", NOTICE, false},
186	{"warning", WARNING, false},
187	{"error", ERROR, false},
188	{NULL, 0, false}
189};
190
191StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(intervalstyle_options) == (INTSTYLE_ISO_8601 + 2),
192				 "array length mismatch");
193
194static const struct config_enum_entry log_error_verbosity_options[] = {
195	{"terse", PGERROR_TERSE, false},
196	{"default", PGERROR_DEFAULT, false},
197	{"verbose", PGERROR_VERBOSE, false},
198	{NULL, 0, false}
199};
200
201StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(log_error_verbosity_options) == (PGERROR_VERBOSE + 2),
202				 "array length mismatch");
203
204static const struct config_enum_entry log_statement_options[] = {
205	{"none", LOGSTMT_NONE, false},
206	{"ddl", LOGSTMT_DDL, false},
207	{"mod", LOGSTMT_MOD, false},
208	{"all", LOGSTMT_ALL, false},
209	{NULL, 0, false}
210};
211
212StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(log_statement_options) == (LOGSTMT_ALL + 2),
213				 "array length mismatch");
214
215static const struct config_enum_entry isolation_level_options[] = {

217	{"repeatable read", XACT_REPEATABLE_READ, false},
218	{"read committed", XACT_READ_COMMITTED, false},
219	{"read uncommitted", XACT_READ_UNCOMMITTED, false},
220	{NULL, 0}
221};

265

272	{NULL, 0, false}

278static const struct config_enum_entry xmloption_options[] = {

280	{"document", XMLOPTION_DOCUMENT, false},
281	{NULL, 0, false}

283
284StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(xmloption_options) == (XMLOPTION_CONTENT + 2),
285				 "array length mismatch");

287/*
288 * Although only "on", "off", and "safe_encoding" are documented, we
289 * accept all the likely variants of "on" and "off".
290 */
291static const struct config_enum_entry backslash_quote_options[] = {
292	{"safe_encoding", BACKSLASH_QUOTE_SAFE_ENCODING, false},
293	{"on", BACKSLASH_QUOTE_ON, false},
294	{"off", BACKSLASH_QUOTE_OFF, false},
295	{"true", BACKSLASH_QUOTE_ON, true},
296	{"false", BACKSLASH_QUOTE_OFF, true},
297	{"yes", BACKSLASH_QUOTE_ON, true},
298	{"no", BACKSLASH_QUOTE_OFF, true},
299	{"1", BACKSLASH_QUOTE_ON, true},
300	{"0", BACKSLASH_QUOTE_OFF, true},
301	{NULL, 0, false}
302};
303
304/*
305 * Although only "on", "off", and "auto" are documented, we accept
306 * all the likely variants of "on" and "off".
307 */
308static const struct config_enum_entry compute_query_id_options[] = {
309	{"auto", COMPUTE_QUERY_ID_AUTO, false},
310	{"regress", COMPUTE_QUERY_ID_REGRESS, false},
311	{"on", COMPUTE_QUERY_ID_ON, false},
312	{"off", COMPUTE_QUERY_ID_OFF, false},
313	{"true", COMPUTE_QUERY_ID_ON, true},
314	{"false", COMPUTE_QUERY_ID_OFF, true},
315	{"yes", COMPUTE_QUERY_ID_ON, true},
316	{"no", COMPUTE_QUERY_ID_OFF, true},
317	{"1", COMPUTE_QUERY_ID_ON, true},
318	{"0", COMPUTE_QUERY_ID_OFF, true},
319	{NULL, 0, false}
320};
321
322/*
323 * Although only "on", "off", and "partition" are documented, we
324 * accept all the likely variants of "on" and "off".
325 */
326static const struct config_enum_entry constraint_exclusion_options[] = {
327	{"partition", CONSTRAINT_EXCLUSION_PARTITION, false},
328	{"on", CONSTRAINT_EXCLUSION_ON, false},
329	{"off", CONSTRAINT_EXCLUSION_OFF, false},
330	{"true", CONSTRAINT_EXCLUSION_ON, true},
331	{"false", CONSTRAINT_EXCLUSION_OFF, true},
332	{"yes", CONSTRAINT_EXCLUSION_ON, true},
333	{"no", CONSTRAINT_EXCLUSION_OFF, true},
334	{"1", CONSTRAINT_EXCLUSION_ON, true},
335	{"0", CONSTRAINT_EXCLUSION_OFF, true},
336	{NULL, 0, false}
337};
338
339/*
340 * Although only "on", "off", "remote_apply", "remote_write", and "local" are
341 * documented, we accept all the likely variants of "on" and "off".
342 */
343static const struct config_enum_entry synchronous_commit_options[] = {
344	{"local", SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT_LOCAL_FLUSH, false},
345	{"remote_write", SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT_REMOTE_WRITE, false},
346	{"remote_apply", SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT_REMOTE_APPLY, false},
347	{"on", SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT_ON, false},
348	{"off", SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT_OFF, false},
349	{"true", SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT_ON, true},
350	{"false", SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT_OFF, true},
351	{"yes", SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT_ON, true},
352	{"no", SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT_OFF, true},
353	{"1", SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT_ON, true},
354	{"0", SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT_OFF, true},
355	{NULL, 0, false}
356};
357
358/*
359 * Although only "on", "off", "try" are documented, we accept all the likely
360 * variants of "on" and "off".
361 */
362static const struct config_enum_entry huge_pages_options[] = {

364	{"on", HUGE_PAGES_ON, false},
365	{"try", HUGE_PAGES_TRY, false},
366	{"true", HUGE_PAGES_ON, true},
367	{"false", HUGE_PAGES_OFF, true},
368	{"yes", HUGE_PAGES_ON, true},
369	{"no", HUGE_PAGES_OFF, true},
370	{"1", HUGE_PAGES_ON, true},
371	{"0", HUGE_PAGES_OFF, true},
372	{NULL, 0, false}
373};
374
375static const struct config_enum_entry huge_pages_status_options[] = {
376	{"off", HUGE_PAGES_OFF, false},
377	{"on", HUGE_PAGES_ON, false},
378	{"unknown", HUGE_PAGES_UNKNOWN, false},
379	{NULL, 0, false}
380};

465	{NULL, 0, false}

471	{"lz4", WAL_COMPRESSION_LZ4, false},

487static const struct config_enum_entry file_copy_method_options[] = {
488	{"copy", FILE_COPY_METHOD_COPY, false},

492	{NULL, 0, false}
493};

502
503/*
504 * Options for enum values stored in other modules
505 */
506extern const struct config_enum_entry wal_level_options[];
507extern const struct config_enum_entry archive_mode_options[];
508extern const struct config_enum_entry recovery_target_action_options[];
509extern const struct config_enum_entry wal_sync_method_options[];
510extern const struct config_enum_entry dynamic_shared_memory_options[];
511
512/*
513 * GUC option variables that are exported from this module
514 */
515bool		AllowAlterSystem = true;
516bool		log_duration = false;
517bool		Debug_print_plan = false;
518bool		Debug_print_parse = false;
519bool		Debug_print_rewritten = false;
520bool		Debug_pretty_print = true;
521
522#ifdef DEBUG_NODE_TESTS_ENABLED
523bool		Debug_copy_parse_plan_trees;
524bool		Debug_write_read_parse_plan_trees;
525bool		Debug_raw_expression_coverage_test;
526#endif
527
528bool		log_parser_stats = false;
529bool		log_planner_stats = false;
530bool		log_executor_stats = false;
531bool		log_statement_stats = false;	/* this is sort of all three above
532											 * together */
533bool		log_btree_build_stats = false;
534char	   *event_source;
535
536bool		row_security;
537bool		check_function_bodies = true;
538
539/*
540 * This GUC exists solely for backward compatibility, check its definition for
541 * details.
542 */
543static bool default_with_oids = false;
544
545bool		current_role_is_superuser;
546
547int			log_min_error_statement = ERROR;
548int			log_min_messages = WARNING;
549int			client_min_messages = NOTICE;
550int			log_min_duration_sample = -1;
551int			log_min_duration_statement = -1;
552int			log_parameter_max_length = -1;
553int			log_parameter_max_length_on_error = 0;
554int			log_temp_files = -1;
555double		log_statement_sample_rate = 1.0;
556double		log_xact_sample_rate = 0;
557char	   *backtrace_functions;
558
559int			temp_file_limit = -1;
560
561int			num_temp_buffers = 1024;
562
563char	   *cluster_name = "";
564char	   *ConfigFileName;
565char	   *HbaFileName;
566char	   *IdentFileName;
567char	   *external_pid_file;
568
569char	   *application_name;
570
571int			tcp_keepalives_idle;
572int			tcp_keepalives_interval;
573int			tcp_keepalives_count;
574int			tcp_user_timeout;
575
576/*
577 * SSL renegotiation was been removed in PostgreSQL 9.5, but we tolerate it
578 * being set to zero (meaning never renegotiate) for backward compatibility.
579 * This avoids breaking compatibility with clients that have never supported
580 * renegotiation and therefore always try to zero it.
581 */
582static int	ssl_renegotiation_limit;
583
584/*
585 * This really belongs in pg_shmem.c, but is defined here so that it doesn't
586 * need to be duplicated in all the different implementations of pg_shmem.c.
587 */
588int			huge_pages = HUGE_PAGES_TRY;
589int			huge_page_size;
590int			huge_pages_status = HUGE_PAGES_UNKNOWN;
591
592/*
593 * These variables are all dummies that don't do anything, except in some
594 * cases provide the value for SHOW to display.  The real state is elsewhere
595 * and is kept in sync by assign_hooks.
596 */
597static char *syslog_ident_str;
598static double phony_random_seed;
599static char *client_encoding_string;
600static char *datestyle_string;
601static char *server_encoding_string;
602static char *server_version_string;
603static int	server_version_num;
604static char *debug_io_direct_string;
605static char *restrict_nonsystem_relation_kind_string;
606
607#ifdef HAVE_SYSLOG
608#define	DEFAULT_SYSLOG_FACILITY LOG_LOCAL0
609#else
610#define	DEFAULT_SYSLOG_FACILITY 0
611#endif
612static int	syslog_facility = DEFAULT_SYSLOG_FACILITY;
613
614static char *timezone_string;
615static char *log_timezone_string;
616static char *timezone_abbreviations_string;
617static char *data_directory;
618static char *session_authorization_string;
619static int	max_function_args;
620static int	max_index_keys;
621static int	max_identifier_length;
622static int	block_size;
623static int	segment_size;
624static int	shared_memory_size_mb;
625static int	shared_memory_size_in_huge_pages;
626static int	wal_block_size;
627static int	num_os_semaphores;
628static bool data_checksums;
629static bool integer_datetimes;
630
631#ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
632#define DEFAULT_ASSERT_ENABLED true
633#else
634#define DEFAULT_ASSERT_ENABLED false
635#endif
636static bool assert_enabled = DEFAULT_ASSERT_ENABLED;
637
638static char *recovery_target_timeline_string;
639static char *recovery_target_string;
640static char *recovery_target_xid_string;
641static char *recovery_target_name_string;
642static char *recovery_target_lsn_string;
643
644/* should be static, but commands/variable.c needs to get at this */
645char	   *role_string;
646
647/* should be static, but guc.c needs to get at this */
648bool		in_hot_standby_guc;
649
650
651/*
652 * Displayable names for context types (enum GucContext)
653 *
654 * Note: these strings are deliberately not localized.
655 */
656const char *const GucContext_Names[] =
657{
658	[PGC_INTERNAL] = "internal",
659	[PGC_POSTMASTER] = "postmaster",
660	[PGC_SIGHUP] = "sighup",
661	[PGC_SU_BACKEND] = "superuser-backend",
662	[PGC_BACKEND] = "backend",
663	[PGC_SUSET] = "superuser",
664	[PGC_USERSET] = "user",
665};
666
667StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(GucContext_Names) == (PGC_USERSET + 1),
668				 "array length mismatch");
669
670/*
671 * Displayable names for source types (enum GucSource)
672 *
673 * Note: these strings are deliberately not localized.
674 */
675const char *const GucSource_Names[] =
676{
677	[PGC_S_DEFAULT] = "default",
678	[PGC_S_DYNAMIC_DEFAULT] = "default",
679	[PGC_S_ENV_VAR] = "environment variable",
680	[PGC_S_FILE] = "configuration file",
681	[PGC_S_ARGV] = "command line",
682	[PGC_S_GLOBAL] = "global",
683	[PGC_S_DATABASE] = "database",
684	[PGC_S_USER] = "user",
685	[PGC_S_DATABASE_USER] = "database user",
686	[PGC_S_CLIENT] = "client",
687	[PGC_S_OVERRIDE] = "override",
688	[PGC_S_INTERACTIVE] = "interactive",
689	[PGC_S_TEST] = "test",
690	[PGC_S_SESSION] = "session",
691};
692
693StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(GucSource_Names) == (PGC_S_SESSION + 1),
694				 "array length mismatch");
695
696/*
697 * Displayable names for the groupings defined in enum config_group
698 */
699const char *const config_group_names[] =
700{
701	[UNGROUPED] = gettext_noop("Ungrouped"),
702	[FILE_LOCATIONS] = gettext_noop("File Locations"),
703	[CONN_AUTH_SETTINGS] = gettext_noop("Connections and Authentication / Connection Settings"),
704	[CONN_AUTH_TCP] = gettext_noop("Connections and Authentication / TCP Settings"),
705	[CONN_AUTH_AUTH] = gettext_noop("Connections and Authentication / Authentication"),
706	[CONN_AUTH_SSL] = gettext_noop("Connections and Authentication / SSL"),
707	[RESOURCES_MEM] = gettext_noop("Resource Usage / Memory"),
708	[RESOURCES_DISK] = gettext_noop("Resource Usage / Disk"),
709	[RESOURCES_KERNEL] = gettext_noop("Resource Usage / Kernel Resources"),
710	[RESOURCES_BGWRITER] = gettext_noop("Resource Usage / Background Writer"),
711	[RESOURCES_IO] = gettext_noop("Resource Usage / I/O"),
712	[RESOURCES_WORKER_PROCESSES] = gettext_noop("Resource Usage / Worker Processes"),
713	[WAL_SETTINGS] = gettext_noop("Write-Ahead Log / Settings"),
714	[WAL_CHECKPOINTS] = gettext_noop("Write-Ahead Log / Checkpoints"),
715	[WAL_ARCHIVING] = gettext_noop("Write-Ahead Log / Archiving"),
716	[WAL_RECOVERY] = gettext_noop("Write-Ahead Log / Recovery"),
717	[WAL_ARCHIVE_RECOVERY] = gettext_noop("Write-Ahead Log / Archive Recovery"),
718	[WAL_RECOVERY_TARGET] = gettext_noop("Write-Ahead Log / Recovery Target"),
719	[WAL_SUMMARIZATION] = gettext_noop("Write-Ahead Log / Summarization"),
720	[REPLICATION_SENDING] = gettext_noop("Replication / Sending Servers"),
721	[REPLICATION_PRIMARY] = gettext_noop("Replication / Primary Server"),
722	[REPLICATION_STANDBY] = gettext_noop("Replication / Standby Servers"),
723	[REPLICATION_SUBSCRIBERS] = gettext_noop("Replication / Subscribers"),
724	[QUERY_TUNING_METHOD] = gettext_noop("Query Tuning / Planner Method Configuration"),
725	[QUERY_TUNING_COST] = gettext_noop("Query Tuning / Planner Cost Constants"),
726	[QUERY_TUNING_GEQO] = gettext_noop("Query Tuning / Genetic Query Optimizer"),
727	[QUERY_TUNING_OTHER] = gettext_noop("Query Tuning / Other Planner Options"),
728	[LOGGING_WHERE] = gettext_noop("Reporting and Logging / Where to Log"),
729	[LOGGING_WHEN] = gettext_noop("Reporting and Logging / When to Log"),
730	[LOGGING_WHAT] = gettext_noop("Reporting and Logging / What to Log"),
731	[PROCESS_TITLE] = gettext_noop("Reporting and Logging / Process Title"),
732	[STATS_MONITORING] = gettext_noop("Statistics / Monitoring"),
733	[STATS_CUMULATIVE] = gettext_noop("Statistics / Cumulative Query and Index Statistics"),
734	[VACUUM_AUTOVACUUM] = gettext_noop("Vacuuming / Automatic Vacuuming"),
735	[VACUUM_COST_DELAY] = gettext_noop("Vacuuming / Cost-Based Vacuum Delay"),
736	[VACUUM_DEFAULT] = gettext_noop("Vacuuming / Default Behavior"),
737	[VACUUM_FREEZING] = gettext_noop("Vacuuming / Freezing"),
738	[CLIENT_CONN_STATEMENT] = gettext_noop("Client Connection Defaults / Statement Behavior"),
739	[CLIENT_CONN_LOCALE] = gettext_noop("Client Connection Defaults / Locale and Formatting"),
740	[CLIENT_CONN_PRELOAD] = gettext_noop("Client Connection Defaults / Shared Library Preloading"),
741	[CLIENT_CONN_OTHER] = gettext_noop("Client Connection Defaults / Other Defaults"),
742	[LOCK_MANAGEMENT] = gettext_noop("Lock Management"),
743	[COMPAT_OPTIONS_PREVIOUS] = gettext_noop("Version and Platform Compatibility / Previous PostgreSQL Versions"),
744	[COMPAT_OPTIONS_OTHER] = gettext_noop("Version and Platform Compatibility / Other Platforms and Clients"),
745	[ERROR_HANDLING_OPTIONS] = gettext_noop("Error Handling"),
746	[PRESET_OPTIONS] = gettext_noop("Preset Options"),
747	[CUSTOM_OPTIONS] = gettext_noop("Customized Options"),
748	[DEVELOPER_OPTIONS] = gettext_noop("Developer Options"),
749};
750
751StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(config_group_names) == (DEVELOPER_OPTIONS + 1),
752				 "array length mismatch");
753
754/*
755 * Displayable names for GUC variable types (enum config_type)
756 *
757 * Note: these strings are deliberately not localized.
758 */
759const char *const config_type_names[] =
760{
761	[PGC_BOOL] = "bool",
762	[PGC_INT] = "integer",
763	[PGC_REAL] = "real",
764	[PGC_STRING] = "string",
765	[PGC_ENUM] = "enum",
766};
767
768StaticAssertDecl(lengthof(config_type_names) == (PGC_ENUM + 1),
769				 "array length mismatch");
770
771
772/*
773 * Contents of GUC tables
774 *
775 * See src/backend/utils/misc/README for design notes.
776 *
777 * TO ADD AN OPTION:
778 *
779 * 1. Declare a global variable of type bool, int, double, or char*
780 *	  and make use of it.
781 *
782 * 2. Decide at what times it's safe to set the option. See guc.h for
783 *	  details.
784 *
785 * 3. Decide on a name, a default value, upper and lower bounds (if
786 *	  applicable), etc.
787 *
788 * 4. Add a record below.
789 *
790 * 5. Add it to src/backend/utils/misc/postgresql.conf.sample, if
791 *	  appropriate.
792 *
793 * 6. Don't forget to document the option (at least in config.sgml).
794 *
795 * 7. If it's a new GUC_LIST_QUOTE option, you must add it to
796 *	  variable_is_guc_list_quote() in src/bin/pg_dump/dumputils.c.
797 */
798
799struct config_bool ConfigureNamesBool[] =
800{
801	{
802		{"enable_seqscan", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
803			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of sequential-scan plans."),
804			NULL,
805			GUC_EXPLAIN
806		},
807		&enable_seqscan,
808		true,
809		NULL, NULL, NULL
810	},
811	{
812		{"enable_indexscan", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
813			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of index-scan plans."),
814			NULL,
815			GUC_EXPLAIN
816		},
817		&enable_indexscan,
818		true,
819		NULL, NULL, NULL
820	},
821	{
822		{"enable_indexonlyscan", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
823			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of index-only-scan plans."),
824			NULL,
825			GUC_EXPLAIN
826		},
827		&enable_indexonlyscan,
828		true,
829		NULL, NULL, NULL
830	},
831	{
832		{"enable_bitmapscan", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
833			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of bitmap-scan plans."),
834			NULL,
835			GUC_EXPLAIN
836		},
837		&enable_bitmapscan,
838		true,
839		NULL, NULL, NULL
840	},
841	{
842		{"enable_tidscan", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
843			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of TID scan plans."),
844			NULL,
845			GUC_EXPLAIN
846		},
847		&enable_tidscan,
848		true,
849		NULL, NULL, NULL
850	},
851	{
852		{"enable_sort", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
853			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of explicit sort steps."),
854			NULL,
855			GUC_EXPLAIN
856		},
857		&enable_sort,
858		true,
859		NULL, NULL, NULL
860	},
861	{
862		{"enable_incremental_sort", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
863			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of incremental sort steps."),
864			NULL,
865			GUC_EXPLAIN
866		},
867		&enable_incremental_sort,
868		true,
869		NULL, NULL, NULL
870	},
871	{
872		{"enable_hashagg", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
873			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of hashed aggregation plans."),
874			NULL,
875			GUC_EXPLAIN
876		},
877		&enable_hashagg,
878		true,
879		NULL, NULL, NULL
880	},
881	{
882		{"enable_material", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
883			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of materialization."),
884			NULL,
885			GUC_EXPLAIN
886		},
887		&enable_material,
888		true,
889		NULL, NULL, NULL
890	},
891	{
892		{"enable_memoize", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
893			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of memoization."),
894			NULL,
895			GUC_EXPLAIN
896		},
897		&enable_memoize,
898		true,
899		NULL, NULL, NULL
900	},
901	{
902		{"enable_nestloop", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
903			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of nested-loop join plans."),
904			NULL,
905			GUC_EXPLAIN
906		},
907		&enable_nestloop,
908		true,
909		NULL, NULL, NULL
910	},
911	{
912		{"enable_mergejoin", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
913			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of merge join plans."),
914			NULL,
915			GUC_EXPLAIN
916		},
917		&enable_mergejoin,
918		true,
919		NULL, NULL, NULL
920	},
921	{
922		{"enable_hashjoin", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
923			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of hash join plans."),
924			NULL,
925			GUC_EXPLAIN
926		},
927		&enable_hashjoin,
928		true,
929		NULL, NULL, NULL
930	},
931	{
932		{"enable_gathermerge", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
933			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of gather merge plans."),
934			NULL,
935			GUC_EXPLAIN
936		},
937		&enable_gathermerge,
938		true,
939		NULL, NULL, NULL
940	},
941	{
942		{"enable_partitionwise_join", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
943			gettext_noop("Enables partitionwise join."),
944			NULL,
945			GUC_EXPLAIN
946		},
947		&enable_partitionwise_join,
948		false,
949		NULL, NULL, NULL
950	},
951	{
952		{"enable_partitionwise_aggregate", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
953			gettext_noop("Enables partitionwise aggregation and grouping."),
954			NULL,
955			GUC_EXPLAIN
956		},
957		&enable_partitionwise_aggregate,
958		false,
959		NULL, NULL, NULL
960	},
961	{
962		{"enable_parallel_append", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
963			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of parallel append plans."),
964			NULL,
965			GUC_EXPLAIN
966		},
967		&enable_parallel_append,
968		true,
969		NULL, NULL, NULL
970	},
971	{
972		{"enable_parallel_hash", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
973			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of parallel hash plans."),
974			NULL,
975			GUC_EXPLAIN
976		},
977		&enable_parallel_hash,
978		true,
979		NULL, NULL, NULL
980	},
981	{
982		{"enable_partition_pruning", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
983			gettext_noop("Enables plan-time and execution-time partition pruning."),
984			gettext_noop("Allows the query planner and executor to compare partition "
985						 "bounds to conditions in the query to determine which "
986						 "partitions must be scanned."),
987			GUC_EXPLAIN
988		},
989		&enable_partition_pruning,
990		true,
991		NULL, NULL, NULL
992	},
993	{
994		{"enable_presorted_aggregate", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
995			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's ability to produce plans that "
996						 "provide presorted input for ORDER BY / DISTINCT aggregate "
997						 "functions."),
998			gettext_noop("Allows the query planner to build plans that provide "
999						 "presorted input for aggregate functions with an ORDER BY / "
1000						 "DISTINCT clause.  When disabled, implicit sorts are always "
1001						 "performed during execution."),
1002			GUC_EXPLAIN
1003		},
1004		&enable_presorted_aggregate,
1005		true,
1006		NULL, NULL, NULL
1007	},
1008	{
1009		{"enable_async_append", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
1010			gettext_noop("Enables the planner's use of async append plans."),
1011			NULL,
1012			GUC_EXPLAIN
1013		},
1014		&enable_async_append,
1015		true,
1016		NULL, NULL, NULL
1017	},
1018	{
1019		{"enable_self_join_elimination", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
1020			gettext_noop("Enables removal of unique self-joins."),
1021			NULL,
1022			GUC_EXPLAIN
1023		},
1024		&enable_self_join_elimination,
1025		true,
1026		NULL, NULL, NULL
1027	},
1028	{
1029		{"enable_group_by_reordering", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
1030			gettext_noop("Enables reordering of GROUP BY keys."),
1031			NULL,
1032			GUC_EXPLAIN
1033		},
1034		&enable_group_by_reordering,
1035		true,
1036		NULL, NULL, NULL
1037	},
1038	{
1039		{"enable_distinct_reordering", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_METHOD,
1040			gettext_noop("Enables reordering of DISTINCT keys."),
1041			NULL,
1042			GUC_EXPLAIN
1043		},
1044		&enable_distinct_reordering,
1045		true,
1046		NULL, NULL, NULL
1047	},
1048	{
1049		{"geqo", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_GEQO,
1050			gettext_noop("Enables genetic query optimization."),
1051			gettext_noop("This algorithm attempts to do planning without "
1052						 "exhaustive searching."),
1053			GUC_EXPLAIN
1054		},
1055		&enable_geqo,
1056		true,
1057		NULL, NULL, NULL
1058	},
1059	{
1060		/*
1061		 * Not for general use --- used by SET SESSION AUTHORIZATION and SET
1062		 * ROLE
1063		 */
1064		{"is_superuser", PGC_INTERNAL, UNGROUPED,
1065			gettext_noop("Shows whether the current user is a superuser."),
1066			NULL,
1067			GUC_REPORT | GUC_NO_SHOW_ALL | GUC_NO_RESET_ALL | GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE | GUC_DISALLOW_IN_FILE | GUC_ALLOW_IN_PARALLEL
1068		},
1069		&current_role_is_superuser,
1070		false,
1071		NULL, NULL, NULL
1072	},
1073	{
1074		/*
1075		 * This setting itself cannot be set by ALTER SYSTEM to avoid an
1076		 * operator turning this setting off by using ALTER SYSTEM, without a
1077		 * way to turn it back on.
1078		 */
1079		{"allow_alter_system", PGC_SIGHUP, COMPAT_OPTIONS_OTHER,
1080			gettext_noop("Allows running the ALTER SYSTEM command."),
1081			gettext_noop("Can be set to off for environments where global configuration "
1082						 "changes should be made using a different method."),
1083			GUC_DISALLOW_IN_AUTO_FILE
1084		},
1085		&AllowAlterSystem,
1086		true,
1087		NULL, NULL, NULL
1088	},
1089	{
1090		{"bonjour", PGC_POSTMASTER, CONN_AUTH_SETTINGS,
1091			gettext_noop("Enables advertising the server via Bonjour."),
1092			NULL
1093		},
1094		&enable_bonjour,
1095		false,
1096		check_bonjour, NULL, NULL
1097	},
1098	{
1099		{"track_commit_timestamp", PGC_POSTMASTER, REPLICATION_SENDING,
1100			gettext_noop("Collects transaction commit time."),
1101			NULL
1102		},
1103		&track_commit_timestamp,
1104		false,
1105		NULL, NULL, NULL
1106	},
1107	{
1108		{"ssl", PGC_SIGHUP, CONN_AUTH_SSL,
1109			gettext_noop("Enables SSL connections."),
1110			NULL
1111		},
1112		&EnableSSL,
1113		false,
1114		check_ssl, NULL, NULL
1115	},
1116	{
1117		{"ssl_passphrase_command_supports_reload", PGC_SIGHUP, CONN_AUTH_SSL,
1118			gettext_noop("Controls whether \"ssl_passphrase_command\" is called during server reload."),
1119			NULL
1120		},
1121		&ssl_passphrase_command_supports_reload,
1122		false,
1123		NULL, NULL, NULL
1124	},
1125	{
1126		{"ssl_prefer_server_ciphers", PGC_SIGHUP, CONN_AUTH_SSL,
1127			gettext_noop("Give priority to server ciphersuite order."),
1128			NULL
1129		},
1130		&SSLPreferServerCiphers,
1131		true,
1132		NULL, NULL, NULL
1133	},
1134	{
1135		{"fsync", PGC_SIGHUP, WAL_SETTINGS,
1136			gettext_noop("Forces synchronization of updates to disk."),
1137			gettext_noop("The server will use the fsync() system call in several places to make "
1138						 "sure that updates are physically written to disk. This ensures "
1139						 "that a database cluster will recover to a consistent state after "
1140						 "an operating system or hardware crash.")
1141		},
1142		&enableFsync,
1143		true,
1144		NULL, NULL, NULL
1145	},
1146	{
1147		{"ignore_checksum_failure", PGC_SUSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
1148			gettext_noop("Continues processing after a checksum failure."),
1149			gettext_noop("Detection of a checksum failure normally causes PostgreSQL to "
1150						 "report an error, aborting the current transaction. Setting "
1151						 "ignore_checksum_failure to true causes the system to ignore the failure "
1152						 "(but still report a warning), and continue processing. This "
1153						 "behavior could cause crashes or other serious problems. Only "
1154						 "has an effect if checksums are enabled."),
1155			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1156		},
1157		&ignore_checksum_failure,
1158		false,
1159		NULL, NULL, NULL
1160	},
1161	{
1162		{"zero_damaged_pages", PGC_SUSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
1163			gettext_noop("Continues processing past damaged page headers."),
1164			gettext_noop("Detection of a damaged page header normally causes PostgreSQL to "
1165						 "report an error, aborting the current transaction. Setting "
1166						 "\"zero_damaged_pages\" to true causes the system to instead report a "
1167						 "warning, zero out the damaged page, and continue processing. This "
1168						 "behavior will destroy data, namely all the rows on the damaged page."),
1169			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1170		},
1171		&zero_damaged_pages,
1172		false,
1173		NULL, NULL, NULL
1174	},
1175	{
1176		{"ignore_invalid_pages", PGC_POSTMASTER, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
1177			gettext_noop("Continues recovery after an invalid pages failure."),
1178			gettext_noop("Detection of WAL records having references to "
1179						 "invalid pages during recovery causes PostgreSQL to "
1180						 "raise a PANIC-level error, aborting the recovery. "
1181						 "Setting \"ignore_invalid_pages\" to true causes "
1182						 "the system to ignore invalid page references "
1183						 "in WAL records (but still report a warning), "
1184						 "and continue recovery. This behavior may cause "
1185						 "crashes, data loss, propagate or hide corruption, "
1186						 "or other serious problems. Only has an effect "
1187						 "during recovery or in standby mode."),
1188			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1189		},
1190		&ignore_invalid_pages,
1191		false,
1192		NULL, NULL, NULL
1193	},
1194	{
1195		{"full_page_writes", PGC_SIGHUP, WAL_SETTINGS,
1196			gettext_noop("Writes full pages to WAL when first modified after a checkpoint."),
1197			gettext_noop("A page write in process during an operating system crash might be "
1198						 "only partially written to disk.  During recovery, the row changes "
1199						 "stored in WAL are not enough to recover.  This option writes "
1200						 "pages when first modified after a checkpoint to WAL so full recovery "
1201						 "is possible.")
1202		},
1203		&fullPageWrites,
1204		true,
1205		NULL, NULL, NULL
1206	},
1207
1208	{
1209		{"wal_log_hints", PGC_POSTMASTER, WAL_SETTINGS,
1210			gettext_noop("Writes full pages to WAL when first modified after a checkpoint, even for a non-critical modification."),
1211			NULL
1212		},
1213		&wal_log_hints,
1214		false,
1215		NULL, NULL, NULL
1216	},
1217
1218	{
1219		{"wal_init_zero", PGC_SUSET, WAL_SETTINGS,
1220			gettext_noop("Writes zeroes to new WAL files before first use."),
1221			NULL
1222		},
1223		&wal_init_zero,
1224		true,
1225		NULL, NULL, NULL
1226	},
1227

1232		},
1233		&wal_recycle,

1235		NULL, NULL, NULL
1236	},

1242		},

1245		NULL, NULL, NULL
1246	},

1252		},

1255		NULL, NULL, NULL
1256	},

1261		},

1265	},

1284	},

1289			NULL
1290		},
1291		&ExitOnAnyError,
1292		false,
1293		NULL, NULL, NULL
1294	},
1295	{
1296		{"restart_after_crash", PGC_SIGHUP, ERROR_HANDLING_OPTIONS,

1298			NULL
1299		},
1300		&restart_after_crash,
1301		true,
1302		NULL, NULL, NULL
1303	},
1304	{
1305		{"remove_temp_files_after_crash", PGC_SIGHUP, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
1306			gettext_noop("Remove temporary files after backend crash."),
1307			NULL,
1308			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1309		},
1310		&remove_temp_files_after_crash,
1311		true,
1312		NULL, NULL, NULL
1313	},
1314	{
1315		{"send_abort_for_crash", PGC_SIGHUP, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,

1317			NULL,
1318			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1319		},
1320		&send_abort_for_crash,
1321		false,
1322		NULL, NULL, NULL
1323	},
1324	{
1325		{"send_abort_for_kill", PGC_SIGHUP, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,

1327			NULL,
1328			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1329		},
1330		&send_abort_for_kill,
1331		false,
1332		NULL, NULL, NULL
1333	},
1334
1335	{
1336		{"log_duration", PGC_SUSET, LOGGING_WHAT,
1337			gettext_noop("Logs the duration of each completed SQL statement."),
1338			NULL
1339		},
1340		&log_duration,
1341		false,
1342		NULL, NULL, NULL
1343	},
1344#ifdef DEBUG_NODE_TESTS_ENABLED
1345	{
1346		{"debug_copy_parse_plan_trees", PGC_SUSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
1347			gettext_noop("Set this to force all parse and plan trees to be passed through "
1348						 "copyObject(), to facilitate catching errors and omissions in "
1349						 "copyObject()."),
1350			NULL,
1351			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1352		},
1353		&Debug_copy_parse_plan_trees,
1354/* support for legacy compile-time setting */
1355#ifdef COPY_PARSE_PLAN_TREES
1356		true,
1357#else
1358		false,
1359#endif
1360		NULL, NULL, NULL
1361	},
1362	{
1363		{"debug_write_read_parse_plan_trees", PGC_SUSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
1364			gettext_noop("Set this to force all parse and plan trees to be passed through "
1365						 "outfuncs.c/readfuncs.c, to facilitate catching errors and omissions in "
1366						 "those modules."),
1367			NULL,
1368			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1369		},
1370		&Debug_write_read_parse_plan_trees,
1371/* support for legacy compile-time setting */
1372#ifdef WRITE_READ_PARSE_PLAN_TREES
1373		true,
1374#else
1375		false,
1376#endif
1377		NULL, NULL, NULL
1378	},
1379	{
1380		{"debug_raw_expression_coverage_test", PGC_SUSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
1381			gettext_noop("Set this to force all raw parse trees for DML statements to be scanned "
1382						 "by raw_expression_tree_walker(), to facilitate catching errors and "
1383						 "omissions in that function."),
1384			NULL,
1385			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1386		},
1387		&Debug_raw_expression_coverage_test,
1388/* support for legacy compile-time setting */
1389#ifdef RAW_EXPRESSION_COVERAGE_TEST
1390		true,
1391#else
1392		false,
1393#endif
1394		NULL, NULL, NULL
1395	},
1396#endif							/* DEBUG_NODE_TESTS_ENABLED */
1397	{

1400			NULL
1401		},

1403		false,
1404		NULL, NULL, NULL
1405	},

1437		},

1439		false,
1440		check_stage_log_stats, NULL, NULL
1441	},

1450	},

1459	},

1491		NULL, NULL, NULL
1492	},

1617		},

1619		false,

1621	},

1624			gettext_noop("Logs standby recovery conflict waits."),
1625			NULL
1626		},
1627		&log_recovery_conflict_waits,
1628		false,
1629		NULL, NULL, NULL
1630	},
1631	{
1632		{"log_hostname", PGC_SIGHUP, LOGGING_WHAT,
1633			gettext_noop("Logs the host name in the connection logs."),
1634			gettext_noop("By default, connection logs only show the IP address "
1635						 "of the connecting host. If you want them to show the host name you "
1636						 "can turn this on, but depending on your host name resolution "
1637						 "setup it might impose a non-negligible performance penalty.")
1638		},
1639		&log_hostname,
1640		false,
1641		NULL, NULL, NULL
1642	},
1643	{
1644		{"transform_null_equals", PGC_USERSET, COMPAT_OPTIONS_OTHER,
1645			gettext_noop("Treats \"expr=NULL\" as \"expr IS NULL\"."),
1646			gettext_noop("When turned on, expressions of the form expr = NULL "
1647						 "(or NULL = expr) are treated as expr IS NULL, that is, they "
1648						 "return true if expr evaluates to the null value, and false "
1649						 "otherwise. The correct behavior of expr = NULL is to always "
1650						 "return null (unknown).")
1651		},
1652		&Transform_null_equals,
1653		false,
1654		NULL, NULL, NULL
1655	},
1656	{
1657		{"default_transaction_read_only", PGC_USERSET, CLIENT_CONN_STATEMENT,
1658			gettext_noop("Sets the default read-only status of new transactions."),
1659			NULL,
1660			GUC_REPORT
1661		},
1662		&DefaultXactReadOnly,
1663		false,
1664		NULL, NULL, NULL
1665	},
1666	{
1667		{"transaction_read_only", PGC_USERSET, CLIENT_CONN_STATEMENT,
1668			gettext_noop("Sets the current transaction's read-only status."),
1669			NULL,
1670			GUC_NO_RESET | GUC_NO_RESET_ALL | GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE | GUC_DISALLOW_IN_FILE
1671		},
1672		&XactReadOnly,
1673		false,
1674		check_transaction_read_only, NULL, NULL
1675	},
1676	{
1677		{"default_transaction_deferrable", PGC_USERSET, CLIENT_CONN_STATEMENT,
1678			gettext_noop("Sets the default deferrable status of new transactions."),
1679			NULL
1680		},
1681		&DefaultXactDeferrable,
1682		false,
1683		NULL, NULL, NULL
1684	},
1685	{
1686		{"transaction_deferrable", PGC_USERSET, CLIENT_CONN_STATEMENT,
1687			gettext_noop("Whether to defer a read-only serializable transaction until it can be executed with no possible serialization failures."),
1688			NULL,
1689			GUC_NO_RESET | GUC_NO_RESET_ALL | GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE | GUC_DISALLOW_IN_FILE
1690		},
1691		&XactDeferrable,
1692		false,
1693		check_transaction_deferrable, NULL, NULL
1694	},
1695	{
1696		{"row_security", PGC_USERSET, CLIENT_CONN_STATEMENT,
1697			gettext_noop("Enables row security."),
1698			gettext_noop("When enabled, row security will be applied to all users.")
1699		},
1700		&row_security,
1701		true,
1702		NULL, NULL, NULL
1703	},
1704	{
1705		{"check_function_bodies", PGC_USERSET, CLIENT_CONN_STATEMENT,
1706			gettext_noop("Check routine bodies during CREATE FUNCTION and CREATE PROCEDURE."),
1707			NULL
1708		},
1709		&check_function_bodies,
1710		true,
1711		NULL, NULL, NULL
1712	},
1713	{
1714		{"array_nulls", PGC_USERSET, COMPAT_OPTIONS_PREVIOUS,
1715			gettext_noop("Enables input of NULL elements in arrays."),
1716			gettext_noop("When turned on, unquoted NULL in an array input "
1717						 "value means a null value; "
1718						 "otherwise it is taken literally.")
1719		},
1720		&Array_nulls,
1721		true,
1722		NULL, NULL, NULL
1723	},
1724
1725	/*
1726	 * WITH OIDS support, and consequently default_with_oids, was removed in
1727	 * PostgreSQL 12, but we tolerate the parameter being set to false to
1728	 * avoid unnecessarily breaking older dump files.
1729	 */
1730	{
1731		{"default_with_oids", PGC_USERSET, COMPAT_OPTIONS_PREVIOUS,
1732			gettext_noop("WITH OIDS is no longer supported; this can only be false."),
1733			NULL,
1734			GUC_NO_SHOW_ALL | GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1735		},
1736		&default_with_oids,
1737		false,
1738		check_default_with_oids, NULL, NULL
1739	},
1740	{
1741		{"logging_collector", PGC_POSTMASTER, LOGGING_WHERE,
1742			gettext_noop("Start a subprocess to capture stderr, csvlog and/or jsonlog into log files."),
1743			NULL
1744		},
1745		&Logging_collector,
1746		false,
1747		NULL, NULL, NULL
1748	},
1749	{
1750		{"log_truncate_on_rotation", PGC_SIGHUP, LOGGING_WHERE,

1752			NULL
1753		},
1754		&Log_truncate_on_rotation,
1755		false,
1756		NULL, NULL, NULL
1757	},

1872	},
1873
1874	{
1875		{"recovery_target_inclusive", PGC_POSTMASTER, WAL_RECOVERY_TARGET,
1876			gettext_noop("Sets whether to include or exclude transaction with recovery target."),
1877			NULL
1878		},
1879		&recoveryTargetInclusive,
1880		true,
1881		NULL, NULL, NULL
1882	},
1883
1884	{
1885		{"summarize_wal", PGC_SIGHUP, WAL_SUMMARIZATION,
1886			gettext_noop("Starts the WAL summarizer process to enable incremental backup."),
1887			NULL
1888		},
1889		&summarize_wal,
1890		false,
1891		NULL, NULL, NULL
1892	},
1893
1894	{
1895		{"hot_standby", PGC_POSTMASTER, REPLICATION_STANDBY,
1896			gettext_noop("Allows connections and queries during recovery."),
1897			NULL
1898		},
1899		&EnableHotStandby,
1900		true,
1901		NULL, NULL, NULL
1902	},
1903
1904	{
1905		{"hot_standby_feedback", PGC_SIGHUP, REPLICATION_STANDBY,
1906			gettext_noop("Allows feedback from a hot standby to the primary that will avoid query conflicts."),
1907			NULL
1908		},
1909		&hot_standby_feedback,
1910		false,
1911		NULL, NULL, NULL
1912	},
1913
1914	{
1915		{"in_hot_standby", PGC_INTERNAL, PRESET_OPTIONS,
1916			gettext_noop("Shows whether hot standby is currently active."),
1917			NULL,
1918			GUC_REPORT | GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE | GUC_DISALLOW_IN_FILE
1919		},
1920		&in_hot_standby_guc,
1921		false,
1922		NULL, NULL, show_in_hot_standby
1923	},
1924
1925	{
1926		{"allow_system_table_mods", PGC_SUSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
1927			gettext_noop("Allows modifications of the structure of system tables."),
1928			NULL,
1929			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1930		},
1931		&allowSystemTableMods,
1932		false,
1933		NULL, NULL, NULL
1934	},
1935
1936	{
1937		{"ignore_system_indexes", PGC_BACKEND, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
1938			gettext_noop("Disables reading from system indexes."),
1939			gettext_noop("It does not prevent updating the indexes, so it is safe "
1940						 "to use.  The worst consequence is slowness."),
1941			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1942		},
1943		&IgnoreSystemIndexes,
1944		false,
1945		NULL, NULL, NULL
1946	},
1947
1948	{
1949		{"allow_in_place_tablespaces", PGC_SUSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
1950			gettext_noop("Allows tablespaces directly inside pg_tblspc, for testing."),
1951			NULL,
1952			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
1953		},
1954		&allow_in_place_tablespaces,
1955		false,
1956		NULL, NULL, NULL
1957	},
1958
1959	{
1960		{"lo_compat_privileges", PGC_SUSET, COMPAT_OPTIONS_PREVIOUS,
1961			gettext_noop("Enables backward compatibility mode for privilege checks on large objects."),
1962			gettext_noop("Skips privilege checks when reading or modifying large objects, "
1963						 "for compatibility with PostgreSQL releases prior to 9.0.")
1964		},
1965		&lo_compat_privileges,
1966		false,
1967		NULL, NULL, NULL
1968	},
1969
1970	{
1971		{"quote_all_identifiers", PGC_USERSET, COMPAT_OPTIONS_PREVIOUS,
1972			gettext_noop("When generating SQL fragments, quote all identifiers."),
1973			NULL,
1974		},
1975		&quote_all_identifiers,
1976		false,
1977		NULL, NULL, NULL
1978	},
1979
1980	{
1981		{"data_checksums", PGC_INTERNAL, PRESET_OPTIONS,
1982			gettext_noop("Shows whether data checksums are turned on for this cluster."),
1983			NULL,
1984			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE | GUC_DISALLOW_IN_FILE | GUC_RUNTIME_COMPUTED
1985		},
1986		&data_checksums,
1987		false,
1988		NULL, NULL, NULL
1989	},
1990
1991	{
1992		{"syslog_sequence_numbers", PGC_SIGHUP, LOGGING_WHERE,
1993			gettext_noop("Add sequence number to syslog messages to avoid duplicate suppression."),
1994			NULL
1995		},
1996		&syslog_sequence_numbers,
1997		true,
1998		NULL, NULL, NULL
1999	},
2000
2001	{
2002		{"syslog_split_messages", PGC_SIGHUP, LOGGING_WHERE,

2004			NULL
2005		},
2006		&syslog_split_messages,
2007		true,
2008		NULL, NULL, NULL
2009	},
2010
2011	{
2012		{"parallel_leader_participation", PGC_USERSET, RESOURCES_WORKER_PROCESSES,
2013			gettext_noop("Controls whether Gather and Gather Merge also run subplans."),
2014			gettext_noop("Should gather nodes also run subplans or just gather tuples?"),
2015			GUC_EXPLAIN
2016		},
2017		&parallel_leader_participation,
2018		true,
2019		NULL, NULL, NULL
2020	},
2021
2022	{
2023		{"jit", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_OTHER,
2024			gettext_noop("Allow JIT compilation."),
2025			NULL,
2026			GUC_EXPLAIN
2027		},
2028		&jit_enabled,
2029		true,
2030		NULL, NULL, NULL
2031	},
2032
2033	{
2034		{"jit_debugging_support", PGC_SU_BACKEND, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
2035			gettext_noop("Register JIT-compiled functions with debugger."),
2036			NULL,
2037			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
2038		},
2039		&jit_debugging_support,
2040		false,
2041
2042		/*
2043		 * This is not guaranteed to be available, but given it's a developer
2044		 * oriented option, it doesn't seem worth adding code checking
2045		 * availability.
2046		 */
2047		NULL, NULL, NULL
2048	},
2049
2050	{
2051		{"jit_dump_bitcode", PGC_SUSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
2052			gettext_noop("Write out LLVM bitcode to facilitate JIT debugging."),
2053			NULL,
2054			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
2055		},
2056		&jit_dump_bitcode,
2057		false,
2058		NULL, NULL, NULL
2059	},
2060
2061	{
2062		{"jit_expressions", PGC_USERSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
2063			gettext_noop("Allow JIT compilation of expressions."),
2064			NULL,
2065			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
2066		},
2067		&jit_expressions,
2068		true,
2069		NULL, NULL, NULL
2070	},
2071
2072	{
2073		{"jit_profiling_support", PGC_SU_BACKEND, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
2074			gettext_noop("Register JIT-compiled functions with perf profiler."),
2075			NULL,
2076			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
2077		},
2078		&jit_profiling_support,
2079		false,
2080
2081		/*
2082		 * This is not guaranteed to be available, but given it's a developer
2083		 * oriented option, it doesn't seem worth adding code checking
2084		 * availability.
2085		 */
2086		NULL, NULL, NULL
2087	},
2088
2089	{
2090		{"jit_tuple_deforming", PGC_USERSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
2091			gettext_noop("Allow JIT compilation of tuple deforming."),
2092			NULL,
2093			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
2094		},
2095		&jit_tuple_deforming,
2096		true,
2097		NULL, NULL, NULL
2098	},

2100	{
2101		{"data_sync_retry", PGC_POSTMASTER, ERROR_HANDLING_OPTIONS,

2103		},
2104		&data_sync_retry,
2105		false,
2106		NULL, NULL, NULL
2107	},
2108
2109	{
2110		{"wal_receiver_create_temp_slot", PGC_SIGHUP, REPLICATION_STANDBY,

2112		},
2113		&wal_receiver_create_temp_slot,
2114		false,
2115		NULL, NULL, NULL
2116	},
2117
2118	{
2119		{"event_triggers", PGC_SUSET, CLIENT_CONN_STATEMENT,
2120			gettext_noop("Enables event triggers."),
2121			gettext_noop("When enabled, event triggers will fire for all applicable statements."),
2122		},
2123		&event_triggers,
2124		true,
2125		NULL, NULL, NULL
2126	},
2127
2128	{
2129		{"sync_replication_slots", PGC_SIGHUP, REPLICATION_STANDBY,
2130			gettext_noop("Enables a physical standby to synchronize logical failover replication slots from the primary server."),
2131		},
2132		&sync_replication_slots,
2133		false,
2134		NULL, NULL, NULL
2135	},
2136
2137	{
2138		{"md5_password_warnings", PGC_USERSET, CONN_AUTH_AUTH,
2139			gettext_noop("Enables deprecation warnings for MD5 passwords."),
2140		},
2141		&md5_password_warnings,
2142		true,
2143		NULL, NULL, NULL
2144	},
2145
2146	{
2147		{"vacuum_truncate", PGC_USERSET, VACUUM_DEFAULT,
2148			gettext_noop("Enables vacuum to truncate empty pages at the end of the table."),
2149		},
2150		&vacuum_truncate,
2151		true,
2152		NULL, NULL, NULL
2153	},
2154
2155	/* End-of-list marker */
2156	{
2157		{NULL, 0, 0, NULL, NULL}, NULL, false, NULL, NULL, NULL
2158	}
2159};
2160
2161
2162struct config_int ConfigureNamesInt[] =
2163{
2164	{
2165		{"archive_timeout", PGC_SIGHUP, WAL_ARCHIVING,
2166			gettext_noop("Sets the amount of time to wait before forcing a "
2167						 "switch to the next WAL file."),
2168			gettext_noop("0 disables the timeout."),
2169			GUC_UNIT_S
2170		},
2171		&XLogArchiveTimeout,
2172		0, 0, INT_MAX / 2,
2173		NULL, NULL, NULL
2174	},
2175	{
2176		{"post_auth_delay", PGC_BACKEND, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
2177			gettext_noop("Sets the amount of time to wait after "
2178						 "authentication on connection startup."),
2179			gettext_noop("This allows attaching a debugger to the process."),
2180			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE | GUC_UNIT_S
2181		},
2182		&PostAuthDelay,
2183		0, 0, INT_MAX / 1000000,
2184		NULL, NULL, NULL
2185	},
2186	{
2187		{"default_statistics_target", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_OTHER,
2188			gettext_noop("Sets the default statistics target."),
2189			gettext_noop("This applies to table columns that have not had a "
2190						 "column-specific target set via ALTER TABLE SET STATISTICS.")
2191		},
2192		&default_statistics_target,
2193		100, 1, MAX_STATISTICS_TARGET,
2194		NULL, NULL, NULL
2195	},
2196	{
2197		{"from_collapse_limit", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_OTHER,
2198			gettext_noop("Sets the FROM-list size beyond which subqueries "
2199						 "are not collapsed."),
2200			gettext_noop("The planner will merge subqueries into upper "
2201						 "queries if the resulting FROM list would have no more than "
2202						 "this many items."),
2203			GUC_EXPLAIN
2204		},
2205		&from_collapse_limit,
2206		8, 1, INT_MAX,
2207		NULL, NULL, NULL
2208	},
2209	{
2210		{"join_collapse_limit", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_OTHER,
2211			gettext_noop("Sets the FROM-list size beyond which JOIN "
2212						 "constructs are not flattened."),
2213			gettext_noop("The planner will flatten explicit JOIN "
2214						 "constructs into lists of FROM items whenever a "
2215						 "list of no more than this many items would result."),
2216			GUC_EXPLAIN
2217		},
2218		&join_collapse_limit,
2219		8, 1, INT_MAX,
2220		NULL, NULL, NULL
2221	},
2222	{
2223		{"geqo_threshold", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_GEQO,
2224			gettext_noop("Sets the threshold of FROM items beyond which GEQO is used."),
2225			NULL,
2226			GUC_EXPLAIN
2227		},
2228		&geqo_threshold,
2229		12, 2, INT_MAX,
2230		NULL, NULL, NULL
2231	},
2232	{
2233		{"geqo_effort", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_GEQO,
2234			gettext_noop("GEQO: effort is used to set the default for other GEQO parameters."),
2235			NULL,
2236			GUC_EXPLAIN
2237		},
2238		&Geqo_effort,
2239		DEFAULT_GEQO_EFFORT, MIN_GEQO_EFFORT, MAX_GEQO_EFFORT,
2240		NULL, NULL, NULL
2241	},
2242	{
2243		{"geqo_pool_size", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_GEQO,
2244			gettext_noop("GEQO: number of individuals in the population."),
2245			gettext_noop("0 means use a suitable default value."),
2246			GUC_EXPLAIN
2247		},
2248		&Geqo_pool_size,
2249		0, 0, INT_MAX,
2250		NULL, NULL, NULL
2251	},
2252	{
2253		{"geqo_generations", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_GEQO,
2254			gettext_noop("GEQO: number of iterations of the algorithm."),
2255			gettext_noop("0 means use a suitable default value."),
2256			GUC_EXPLAIN
2257		},
2258		&Geqo_generations,
2259		0, 0, INT_MAX,
2260		NULL, NULL, NULL
2261	},
2262
2263	{
2264		/* This is PGC_SUSET to prevent hiding from log_lock_waits. */
2265		{"deadlock_timeout", PGC_SUSET, LOCK_MANAGEMENT,
2266			gettext_noop("Sets the time to wait on a lock before checking for deadlock."),
2267			NULL,
2268			GUC_UNIT_MS
2269		},
2270		&DeadlockTimeout,
2271		1000, 1, INT_MAX,
2272		NULL, NULL, NULL
2273	},
2274
2275	{
2276		{"max_standby_archive_delay", PGC_SIGHUP, REPLICATION_STANDBY,
2277			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum delay before canceling queries when a hot standby server is processing archived WAL data."),
2278			gettext_noop("-1 means wait forever."),
2279			GUC_UNIT_MS
2280		},
2281		&max_standby_archive_delay,
2282		30 * 1000, -1, INT_MAX,
2283		NULL, NULL, NULL
2284	},
2285
2286	{
2287		{"max_standby_streaming_delay", PGC_SIGHUP, REPLICATION_STANDBY,
2288			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum delay before canceling queries when a hot standby server is processing streamed WAL data."),
2289			gettext_noop("-1 means wait forever."),
2290			GUC_UNIT_MS
2291		},
2292		&max_standby_streaming_delay,
2293		30 * 1000, -1, INT_MAX,
2294		NULL, NULL, NULL
2295	},
2296
2297	{
2298		{"recovery_min_apply_delay", PGC_SIGHUP, REPLICATION_STANDBY,
2299			gettext_noop("Sets the minimum delay for applying changes during recovery."),
2300			NULL,
2301			GUC_UNIT_MS
2302		},
2303		&recovery_min_apply_delay,
2304		0, 0, INT_MAX,
2305		NULL, NULL, NULL
2306	},
2307
2308	{
2309		{"wal_receiver_status_interval", PGC_SIGHUP, REPLICATION_STANDBY,
2310			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum interval between WAL receiver status reports to the sending server."),
2311			NULL,
2312			GUC_UNIT_S
2313		},
2314		&wal_receiver_status_interval,
2315		10, 0, INT_MAX / 1000,
2316		NULL, NULL, NULL
2317	},
2318
2319	{
2320		{"wal_receiver_timeout", PGC_SIGHUP, REPLICATION_STANDBY,
2321			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum wait time to receive data from the sending server."),
2322			gettext_noop("0 disables the timeout."),
2323			GUC_UNIT_MS
2324		},
2325		&wal_receiver_timeout,
2326		60 * 1000, 0, INT_MAX,
2327		NULL, NULL, NULL
2328	},
2329
2330	{
2331		{"max_connections", PGC_POSTMASTER, CONN_AUTH_SETTINGS,
2332			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum number of concurrent connections."),
2333			NULL
2334		},
2335		&MaxConnections,
2336		100, 1, MAX_BACKENDS,
2337		NULL, NULL, NULL
2338	},
2339
2340	{
2341		/* see max_connections */
2342		{"superuser_reserved_connections", PGC_POSTMASTER, CONN_AUTH_SETTINGS,
2343			gettext_noop("Sets the number of connection slots reserved for superusers."),
2344			NULL
2345		},
2346		&SuperuserReservedConnections,
2347		3, 0, MAX_BACKENDS,
2348		NULL, NULL, NULL
2349	},
2350
2351	{
2352		{"reserved_connections", PGC_POSTMASTER, CONN_AUTH_SETTINGS,
2353			gettext_noop("Sets the number of connection slots reserved for roles "
2354						 "with privileges of pg_use_reserved_connections."),
2355			NULL
2356		},
2357		&ReservedConnections,
2358		0, 0, MAX_BACKENDS,
2359		NULL, NULL, NULL
2360	},
2361
2362	{
2363		{"min_dynamic_shared_memory", PGC_POSTMASTER, RESOURCES_MEM,
2364			gettext_noop("Amount of dynamic shared memory reserved at startup."),
2365			NULL,
2366			GUC_UNIT_MB
2367		},
2368		&min_dynamic_shared_memory,
2369		0, 0, (int) Min((size_t) INT_MAX, SIZE_MAX / (1024 * 1024)),
2370		NULL, NULL, NULL
2371	},
2372
2373	/*
2374	 * We sometimes multiply the number of shared buffers by two without
2375	 * checking for overflow, so we mustn't allow more than INT_MAX / 2.
2376	 */
2377	{
2378		{"shared_buffers", PGC_POSTMASTER, RESOURCES_MEM,
2379			gettext_noop("Sets the number of shared memory buffers used by the server."),
2380			NULL,
2381			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS
2382		},
2383		&NBuffers,
2384		16384, 16, INT_MAX / 2,
2385		NULL, NULL, NULL
2386	},
2387
2388	{
2389		{"vacuum_buffer_usage_limit", PGC_USERSET, RESOURCES_MEM,
2390			gettext_noop("Sets the buffer pool size for VACUUM, ANALYZE, and autovacuum."),
2391			NULL,
2392			GUC_UNIT_KB
2393		},
2394		&VacuumBufferUsageLimit,
2395		2048, 0, MAX_BAS_VAC_RING_SIZE_KB,
2396		check_vacuum_buffer_usage_limit, NULL, NULL
2397	},
2398
2399	{
2400		{"shared_memory_size", PGC_INTERNAL, PRESET_OPTIONS,
2401			gettext_noop("Shows the size of the server's main shared memory area (rounded up to the nearest MB)."),
2402			NULL,
2403			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE | GUC_DISALLOW_IN_FILE | GUC_UNIT_MB | GUC_RUNTIME_COMPUTED
2404		},
2405		&shared_memory_size_mb,
2406		0, 0, INT_MAX,
2407		NULL, NULL, NULL
2408	},
2409
2410	{
2411		{"shared_memory_size_in_huge_pages", PGC_INTERNAL, PRESET_OPTIONS,
2412			gettext_noop("Shows the number of huge pages needed for the main shared memory area."),
2413			gettext_noop("-1 means huge pages are not supported."),
2414			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE | GUC_DISALLOW_IN_FILE | GUC_RUNTIME_COMPUTED
2415		},
2416		&shared_memory_size_in_huge_pages,
2417		-1, -1, INT_MAX,
2418		NULL, NULL, NULL
2419	},
2420
2421	{
2422		{"num_os_semaphores", PGC_INTERNAL, PRESET_OPTIONS,
2423			gettext_noop("Shows the number of semaphores required for the server."),
2424			NULL,
2425			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE | GUC_DISALLOW_IN_FILE | GUC_RUNTIME_COMPUTED
2426		},
2427		&num_os_semaphores,
2428		0, 0, INT_MAX,
2429		NULL, NULL, NULL
2430	},
2431
2432	{
2433		{"commit_timestamp_buffers", PGC_POSTMASTER, RESOURCES_MEM,
2434			gettext_noop("Sets the size of the dedicated buffer pool used for the commit timestamp cache."),
2435			gettext_noop("0 means use a fraction of \"shared_buffers\"."),
2436			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS
2437		},
2438		&commit_timestamp_buffers,
2439		0, 0, SLRU_MAX_ALLOWED_BUFFERS,
2440		check_commit_ts_buffers, NULL, NULL
2441	},
2442
2443	{
2444		{"multixact_member_buffers", PGC_POSTMASTER, RESOURCES_MEM,
2445			gettext_noop("Sets the size of the dedicated buffer pool used for the MultiXact member cache."),
2446			NULL,
2447			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS
2448		},
2449		&multixact_member_buffers,
2450		32, 16, SLRU_MAX_ALLOWED_BUFFERS,
2451		check_multixact_member_buffers, NULL, NULL
2452	},
2453
2454	{
2455		{"multixact_offset_buffers", PGC_POSTMASTER, RESOURCES_MEM,
2456			gettext_noop("Sets the size of the dedicated buffer pool used for the MultiXact offset cache."),
2457			NULL,
2458			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS
2459		},
2460		&multixact_offset_buffers,
2461		16, 16, SLRU_MAX_ALLOWED_BUFFERS,
2462		check_multixact_offset_buffers, NULL, NULL
2463	},
2464
2465	{
2466		{"notify_buffers", PGC_POSTMASTER, RESOURCES_MEM,
2467			gettext_noop("Sets the size of the dedicated buffer pool used for the LISTEN/NOTIFY message cache."),
2468			NULL,
2469			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS
2470		},
2471		&notify_buffers,
2472		16, 16, SLRU_MAX_ALLOWED_BUFFERS,
2473		check_notify_buffers, NULL, NULL
2474	},
2475
2476	{
2477		{"serializable_buffers", PGC_POSTMASTER, RESOURCES_MEM,
2478			gettext_noop("Sets the size of the dedicated buffer pool used for the serializable transaction cache."),
2479			NULL,
2480			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS
2481		},
2482		&serializable_buffers,
2483		32, 16, SLRU_MAX_ALLOWED_BUFFERS,
2484		check_serial_buffers, NULL, NULL
2485	},
2486
2487	{
2488		{"subtransaction_buffers", PGC_POSTMASTER, RESOURCES_MEM,
2489			gettext_noop("Sets the size of the dedicated buffer pool used for the subtransaction cache."),
2490			gettext_noop("0 means use a fraction of \"shared_buffers\"."),
2491			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS
2492		},
2493		&subtransaction_buffers,
2494		0, 0, SLRU_MAX_ALLOWED_BUFFERS,
2495		check_subtrans_buffers, NULL, NULL
2496	},
2497
2498	{
2499		{"transaction_buffers", PGC_POSTMASTER, RESOURCES_MEM,
2500			gettext_noop("Sets the size of the dedicated buffer pool used for the transaction status cache."),
2501			gettext_noop("0 means use a fraction of \"shared_buffers\"."),
2502			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS
2503		},
2504		&transaction_buffers,
2505		0, 0, SLRU_MAX_ALLOWED_BUFFERS,
2506		check_transaction_buffers, NULL, NULL
2507	},
2508
2509	{
2510		{"temp_buffers", PGC_USERSET, RESOURCES_MEM,
2511			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum number of temporary buffers used by each session."),
2512			NULL,
2513			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS | GUC_EXPLAIN
2514		},
2515		&num_temp_buffers,
2516		1024, 100, INT_MAX / 2,
2517		check_temp_buffers, NULL, NULL
2518	},
2519
2520	{
2521		{"port", PGC_POSTMASTER, CONN_AUTH_SETTINGS,
2522			gettext_noop("Sets the TCP port the server listens on."),
2523			NULL
2524		},
2525		&PostPortNumber,
2526		DEF_PGPORT, 1, 65535,
2527		NULL, NULL, NULL
2528	},
2529
2530	{
2531		{"unix_socket_permissions", PGC_POSTMASTER, CONN_AUTH_SETTINGS,
2532			gettext_noop("Sets the access permissions of the Unix-domain socket."),
2533			gettext_noop("Unix-domain sockets use the usual Unix file system "
2534						 "permission set. The parameter value is expected "
2535						 "to be a numeric mode specification in the form "
2536						 "accepted by the chmod and umask system calls. "
2537						 "(To use the customary octal format the number must "
2538						 "start with a 0 (zero).)")
2539		},
2540		&Unix_socket_permissions,
2541		0777, 0000, 0777,
2542		NULL, NULL, show_unix_socket_permissions
2543	},
2544
2545	{
2546		{"log_file_mode", PGC_SIGHUP, LOGGING_WHERE,
2547			gettext_noop("Sets the file permissions for log files."),
2548			gettext_noop("The parameter value is expected "
2549						 "to be a numeric mode specification in the form "
2550						 "accepted by the chmod and umask system calls. "
2551						 "(To use the customary octal format the number must "
2552						 "start with a 0 (zero).)")
2553		},
2554		&Log_file_mode,
2555		0600, 0000, 0777,
2556		NULL, NULL, show_log_file_mode
2557	},
2558
2559
2560	{
2561		{"data_directory_mode", PGC_INTERNAL, PRESET_OPTIONS,
2562			gettext_noop("Shows the mode of the data directory."),
2563			gettext_noop("The parameter value is a numeric mode specification "
2564						 "in the form accepted by the chmod and umask system "
2565						 "calls. (To use the customary octal format the number "
2566						 "must start with a 0 (zero).)"),
2567			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE | GUC_DISALLOW_IN_FILE | GUC_RUNTIME_COMPUTED
2568		},
2569		&data_directory_mode,
2570		0700, 0000, 0777,
2571		NULL, NULL, show_data_directory_mode
2572	},
2573
2574	{
2575		{"work_mem", PGC_USERSET, RESOURCES_MEM,
2576			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum memory to be used for query workspaces."),
2577			gettext_noop("This much memory can be used by each internal "
2578						 "sort operation and hash table before switching to "
2579						 "temporary disk files."),
2580			GUC_UNIT_KB | GUC_EXPLAIN
2581		},
2582		&work_mem,
2583		4096, 64, MAX_KILOBYTES,
2584		NULL, NULL, NULL
2585	},
2586
2587	/*
2588	 * Dynamic shared memory has a higher overhead than local memory contexts,
2589	 * so when testing low-memory scenarios that could use shared memory, the
2590	 * recommended minimum is 1MB.
2591	 */
2592	{
2593		{"maintenance_work_mem", PGC_USERSET, RESOURCES_MEM,
2594			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum memory to be used for maintenance operations."),
2595			gettext_noop("This includes operations such as VACUUM and CREATE INDEX."),
2596			GUC_UNIT_KB
2597		},
2598		&maintenance_work_mem,
2599		65536, 64, MAX_KILOBYTES,
2600		NULL, NULL, NULL
2601	},
2602
2603	{
2604		{"logical_decoding_work_mem", PGC_USERSET, RESOURCES_MEM,
2605			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum memory to be used for logical decoding."),
2606			gettext_noop("This much memory can be used by each internal "
2607						 "reorder buffer before spilling to disk."),
2608			GUC_UNIT_KB
2609		},
2610		&logical_decoding_work_mem,
2611		65536, 64, MAX_KILOBYTES,
2612		NULL, NULL, NULL
2613	},
2614
2615	/*
2616	 * We use the hopefully-safely-small value of 100kB as the compiled-in
2617	 * default for max_stack_depth.  InitializeGUCOptions will increase it if
2618	 * possible, depending on the actual platform-specific stack limit.
2619	 */
2620	{
2621		{"max_stack_depth", PGC_SUSET, RESOURCES_MEM,
2622			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum stack depth, in kilobytes."),
2623			NULL,
2624			GUC_UNIT_KB
2625		},
2626		&max_stack_depth,
2627		100, 100, MAX_KILOBYTES,
2628		check_max_stack_depth, assign_max_stack_depth, NULL
2629	},
2630
2631	{
2632		{"temp_file_limit", PGC_SUSET, RESOURCES_DISK,
2633			gettext_noop("Limits the total size of all temporary files used by each process."),
2634			gettext_noop("-1 means no limit."),
2635			GUC_UNIT_KB
2636		},
2637		&temp_file_limit,
2638		-1, -1, INT_MAX,
2639		NULL, NULL, NULL
2640	},

2852	/*
2853	 * See also CheckRequiredParameterValues() if this parameter changes
2854	 */
2855	{
2856		{"max_locks_per_transaction", PGC_POSTMASTER, LOCK_MANAGEMENT,
2857			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum number of locks per transaction."),
2858			gettext_noop("The shared lock table is sized on the assumption that at most "
2859						 "\"max_locks_per_transaction\" objects per server process or prepared "
2860						 "transaction will need to be locked at any one time.")
2861		},
2862		&max_locks_per_xact,
2863		64, 10, INT_MAX,
2864		NULL, NULL, NULL
2865	},
2866
2867	{
2868		{"max_pred_locks_per_transaction", PGC_POSTMASTER, LOCK_MANAGEMENT,
2869			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum number of predicate locks per transaction."),
2870			gettext_noop("The shared predicate lock table is sized on the assumption that "
2871						 "at most \"max_pred_locks_per_transaction\" objects per server process "
2872						 "or prepared transaction will need to be locked at any one time.")
2873		},
2874		&max_predicate_locks_per_xact,
2875		64, 10, INT_MAX,
2876		NULL, NULL, NULL
2877	},
2878
2879	{
2880		{"max_pred_locks_per_relation", PGC_SIGHUP, LOCK_MANAGEMENT,
2881			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum number of predicate-locked pages and tuples per relation."),
2882			gettext_noop("If more than this total of pages and tuples in the same relation are locked "
2883						 "by a connection, those locks are replaced by a relation-level lock.")
2884		},
2885		&max_predicate_locks_per_relation,
2886		-2, INT_MIN, INT_MAX,
2887		NULL, NULL, NULL
2888	},
2889
2890	{
2891		{"max_pred_locks_per_page", PGC_SIGHUP, LOCK_MANAGEMENT,
2892			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum number of predicate-locked tuples per page."),
2893			gettext_noop("If more than this number of tuples on the same page are locked "
2894						 "by a connection, those locks are replaced by a page-level lock.")
2895		},
2896		&max_predicate_locks_per_page,
2897		2, 0, INT_MAX,
2898		NULL, NULL, NULL
2899	},
2900

2951		},

2955	},
2956
2957	{
2958		{"min_wal_size", PGC_SIGHUP, WAL_CHECKPOINTS,
2959			gettext_noop("Sets the minimum size to shrink the WAL to."),
2960			NULL,
2961			GUC_UNIT_MB
2962		},
2963		&min_wal_size_mb,

2967	},
2968

2972			NULL,
2973			GUC_UNIT_MB
2974		},

2977		2, MAX_KILOBYTES,
2978		NULL, assign_max_wal_size, NULL
2979	},
2980

2984			NULL,
2985			GUC_UNIT_S
2986		},
2987		&CheckPointTimeout,
2988		300, 30, 86400,
2989		NULL, NULL, NULL
2990	},
2991

2993		{"checkpoint_warning", PGC_SIGHUP, WAL_CHECKPOINTS,

3000			GUC_UNIT_S
3001		},

3005	},

3012		},

3016	},

3023		},

3027	},

3034		},

3038	},

3065			NULL
3066		},

3069		NULL, NULL, NULL
3070	},

3077		},

3081	},

3083	{
3084		{"max_slot_wal_keep_size", PGC_SIGHUP, REPLICATION_SENDING,
3085			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum WAL size that can be reserved by replication slots."),
3086			gettext_noop("Replication slots will be marked as failed, and segments released "
3087						 "for deletion or recycling, if this much space is occupied by WAL on disk. "
3088						 "-1 means no maximum."),
3089			GUC_UNIT_MB
3090		},
3091		&max_slot_wal_keep_size_mb,

3093		NULL, NULL, NULL
3094	},
3095
3096	{
3097		{"wal_sender_timeout", PGC_USERSET, REPLICATION_SENDING,
3098			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum time to wait for WAL replication."),
3099			NULL,
3100			GUC_UNIT_MS
3101		},
3102		&wal_sender_timeout,
3103		60 * 1000, 0, INT_MAX,
3104		NULL, NULL, NULL
3105	},
3106

3120		{"commit_delay", PGC_SUSET, WAL_SETTINGS,
3121			gettext_noop("Sets the delay in microseconds between transaction commit and "

3123			NULL
3124			/* we have no microseconds designation, so can't supply units here */
3125		},
3126		&CommitDelay,
3127		0, 0, 100000,
3128		NULL, NULL, NULL
3129	},
3130
3131	{
3132		{"commit_siblings", PGC_USERSET, WAL_SETTINGS,

3135			NULL
3136		},
3137		&CommitSiblings,
3138		5, 0, 1000,
3139		NULL, NULL, NULL
3140	},
3141
3142	{
3143		{"extra_float_digits", PGC_USERSET, CLIENT_CONN_LOCALE,
3144			gettext_noop("Sets the number of digits displayed for floating-point values."),
3145			gettext_noop("This affects real, double precision, and geometric data types. "
3146						 "A zero or negative parameter value is added to the standard "
3147						 "number of digits (FLT_DIG or DBL_DIG as appropriate). "
3148						 "Any value greater than zero selects precise output mode.")
3149		},
3150		&extra_float_digits,
3151		1, -15, 3,
3152		NULL, NULL, NULL
3153	},
3154
3155	{
3156		{"log_min_duration_sample", PGC_SUSET, LOGGING_WHEN,
3157			gettext_noop("Sets the minimum execution time above which "
3158						 "a sample of statements will be logged."
3159						 " Sampling is determined by \"log_statement_sample_rate\"."),
3160			gettext_noop("-1 disables sampling. 0 means sample all statements."),
3161			GUC_UNIT_MS
3162		},
3163		&log_min_duration_sample,
3164		-1, -1, INT_MAX,
3165		NULL, NULL, NULL
3166	},
3167
3168	{
3169		{"log_min_duration_statement", PGC_SUSET, LOGGING_WHEN,
3170			gettext_noop("Sets the minimum execution time above which "

3172			gettext_noop("-1 disables logging statement durations. 0 means log all statement durations."),
3173			GUC_UNIT_MS
3174		},
3175		&log_min_duration_statement,
3176		-1, -1, INT_MAX,
3177		NULL, NULL, NULL
3178	},
3179

3189		NULL, NULL, NULL
3190	},
3191

3555	},

3579		},

3581

3585	},

3591		},

3692	{
3693		{"tcp_keepalives_count", PGC_USERSET, CONN_AUTH_TCP,
3694			gettext_noop("Maximum number of TCP keepalive retransmits."),
3695			gettext_noop("Number of consecutive keepalive retransmits that can be "
3696						 "lost before a connection is considered dead. "
3697						 "0 means use the system default."),
3698		},
3699		&tcp_keepalives_count,
3700		0, 0, INT_MAX,
3701		NULL, assign_tcp_keepalives_count, show_tcp_keepalives_count
3702	},
3703
3704	{
3705		{"gin_fuzzy_search_limit", PGC_USERSET, CLIENT_CONN_OTHER,
3706			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum allowed result for exact search by GIN."),
3707			gettext_noop("0 means no limit."),
3708		},
3709		&GinFuzzySearchLimit,
3710		0, 0, INT_MAX,
3711		NULL, NULL, NULL
3712	},
3713
3714	{
3715		{"effective_cache_size", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3716			gettext_noop("Sets the planner's assumption about the total size of the data caches."),
3717			gettext_noop("That is, the total size of the caches (kernel cache and shared buffers) used for PostgreSQL data files. "
3718						 "This is measured in disk pages, which are normally 8 kB each."),
3719			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS | GUC_EXPLAIN,
3720		},
3721		&effective_cache_size,
3722		DEFAULT_EFFECTIVE_CACHE_SIZE, 1, INT_MAX,
3723		NULL, NULL, NULL
3724	},
3725
3726	{
3727		{"min_parallel_table_scan_size", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3728			gettext_noop("Sets the minimum amount of table data for a parallel scan."),
3729			gettext_noop("If the planner estimates that it will read a number of table pages too small to reach this limit, a parallel scan will not be considered."),
3730			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS | GUC_EXPLAIN,
3731		},
3732		&min_parallel_table_scan_size,
3733		(8 * 1024 * 1024) / BLCKSZ, 0, INT_MAX / 3,
3734		NULL, NULL, NULL
3735	},
3736
3737	{
3738		{"min_parallel_index_scan_size", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3739			gettext_noop("Sets the minimum amount of index data for a parallel scan."),
3740			gettext_noop("If the planner estimates that it will read a number of index pages too small to reach this limit, a parallel scan will not be considered."),
3741			GUC_UNIT_BLOCKS | GUC_EXPLAIN,
3742		},
3743		&min_parallel_index_scan_size,
3744		(512 * 1024) / BLCKSZ, 0, INT_MAX / 3,
3745		NULL, NULL, NULL
3746	},
3747
3748	{
3749		/* Can't be set in postgresql.conf */
3750		{"server_version_num", PGC_INTERNAL, PRESET_OPTIONS,
3751			gettext_noop("Shows the server version as an integer."),
3752			NULL,
3753			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE | GUC_DISALLOW_IN_FILE
3754		},
3755		&server_version_num,
3756		PG_VERSION_NUM, PG_VERSION_NUM, PG_VERSION_NUM,
3757		NULL, NULL, NULL
3758	},
3759
3760	{
3761		{"log_temp_files", PGC_SUSET, LOGGING_WHAT,
3762			gettext_noop("Log the use of temporary files larger than this number of kilobytes."),
3763			gettext_noop("-1 disables logging temporary files. 0 means log all temporary files."),
3764			GUC_UNIT_KB
3765		},
3766		&log_temp_files,
3767		-1, -1, INT_MAX,
3768		NULL, NULL, NULL
3769	},
3770
3771	{
3772		{"track_activity_query_size", PGC_POSTMASTER, STATS_CUMULATIVE,
3773			gettext_noop("Sets the size reserved for pg_stat_activity.query, in bytes."),
3774			NULL,
3775			GUC_UNIT_BYTE
3776		},
3777		&pgstat_track_activity_query_size,
3778		1024, 100, 1048576,
3779		NULL, NULL, NULL
3780	},
3781
3782	{
3783		{"gin_pending_list_limit", PGC_USERSET, CLIENT_CONN_STATEMENT,
3784			gettext_noop("Sets the maximum size of the pending list for GIN index."),
3785			NULL,
3786			GUC_UNIT_KB
3787		},
3788		&gin_pending_list_limit,
3789		4096, 64, MAX_KILOBYTES,
3790		NULL, NULL, NULL
3791	},
3792
3793	{
3794		{"tcp_user_timeout", PGC_USERSET, CONN_AUTH_TCP,
3795			gettext_noop("TCP user timeout."),
3796			gettext_noop("0 means use the system default."),
3797			GUC_UNIT_MS
3798		},
3799		&tcp_user_timeout,
3800		0, 0, INT_MAX,
3801		NULL, assign_tcp_user_timeout, show_tcp_user_timeout
3802	},
3803
3804	{
3805		{"huge_page_size", PGC_POSTMASTER, RESOURCES_MEM,
3806			gettext_noop("The size of huge page that should be requested."),
3807			gettext_noop("0 means use the system default."),
3808			GUC_UNIT_KB
3809		},
3810		&huge_page_size,
3811		0, 0, INT_MAX,
3812		check_huge_page_size, NULL, NULL
3813	},
3814
3815	{
3816		{"debug_discard_caches", PGC_SUSET, DEVELOPER_OPTIONS,
3817			gettext_noop("Aggressively flush system caches for debugging purposes."),
3818			gettext_noop("0 means use normal caching behavior."),
3819			GUC_NOT_IN_SAMPLE
3820		},
3821		&debug_discard_caches,
3822#ifdef DISCARD_CACHES_ENABLED
3823		/* Set default based on older compile-time-only cache clobber macros */
3824#if defined(CLOBBER_CACHE_RECURSIVELY)
3825		3,
3826#elif defined(CLOBBER_CACHE_ALWAYS)
3827		1,
3828#else
3829		0,
3830#endif
3831		0, 5,
3832#else							/* not DISCARD_CACHES_ENABLED */
3833		0, 0, 0,
3834#endif							/* not DISCARD_CACHES_ENABLED */
3835		NULL, NULL, NULL
3836	},
3837
3838	{
3839		{"client_connection_check_interval", PGC_USERSET, CONN_AUTH_TCP,
3840			gettext_noop("Sets the time interval between checks for disconnection while running queries."),
3841			gettext_noop("0 disables connection checks."),
3842			GUC_UNIT_MS
3843		},
3844		&client_connection_check_interval,
3845		0, 0, INT_MAX,
3846		check_client_connection_check_interval, NULL, NULL
3847	},
3848
3849	{
3850		{"log_startup_progress_interval", PGC_SIGHUP, LOGGING_WHEN,
3851			gettext_noop("Time between progress updates for "
3852						 "long-running startup operations."),
3853			gettext_noop("0 disables progress updates."),
3854			GUC_UNIT_MS,
3855		},
3856		&log_startup_progress_interval,
3857		10000, 0, INT_MAX,
3858		NULL, NULL, NULL
3859	},
3860
3861	{
3862		{"scram_iterations", PGC_USERSET, CONN_AUTH_AUTH,
3863			gettext_noop("Sets the iteration count for SCRAM secret generation."),
3864			NULL,
3865			GUC_REPORT
3866		},
3867		&scram_sha_256_iterations,
3868		SCRAM_SHA_256_DEFAULT_ITERATIONS, 1, INT_MAX,
3869		NULL, NULL, NULL
3870	},
3871
3872	/* End-of-list marker */
3873	{
3874		{NULL, 0, 0, NULL, NULL}, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, NULL, NULL
3875	}
3876};
3877

3879struct config_real ConfigureNamesReal[] =
3880{
3881	{
3882		{"seq_page_cost", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3883			gettext_noop("Sets the planner's estimate of the cost of a "
3884						 "sequentially fetched disk page."),
3885			NULL,
3886			GUC_EXPLAIN
3887		},
3888		&seq_page_cost,
3889		DEFAULT_SEQ_PAGE_COST, 0, DBL_MAX,
3890		NULL, NULL, NULL
3891	},
3892	{
3893		{"random_page_cost", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3894			gettext_noop("Sets the planner's estimate of the cost of a "
3895						 "nonsequentially fetched disk page."),
3896			NULL,
3897			GUC_EXPLAIN
3898		},
3899		&random_page_cost,
3900		DEFAULT_RANDOM_PAGE_COST, 0, DBL_MAX,
3901		NULL, NULL, NULL
3902	},
3903	{
3904		{"cpu_tuple_cost", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3905			gettext_noop("Sets the planner's estimate of the cost of "
3906						 "processing each tuple (row)."),
3907			NULL,
3908			GUC_EXPLAIN
3909		},
3910		&cpu_tuple_cost,
3911		DEFAULT_CPU_TUPLE_COST, 0, DBL_MAX,
3912		NULL, NULL, NULL
3913	},
3914	{
3915		{"cpu_index_tuple_cost", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3916			gettext_noop("Sets the planner's estimate of the cost of "
3917						 "processing each index entry during an index scan."),
3918			NULL,
3919			GUC_EXPLAIN
3920		},
3921		&cpu_index_tuple_cost,
3922		DEFAULT_CPU_INDEX_TUPLE_COST, 0, DBL_MAX,
3923		NULL, NULL, NULL
3924	},
3925	{
3926		{"cpu_operator_cost", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3927			gettext_noop("Sets the planner's estimate of the cost of "
3928						 "processing each operator or function call."),
3929			NULL,
3930			GUC_EXPLAIN
3931		},
3932		&cpu_operator_cost,
3933		DEFAULT_CPU_OPERATOR_COST, 0, DBL_MAX,
3934		NULL, NULL, NULL
3935	},
3936	{
3937		{"parallel_tuple_cost", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3938			gettext_noop("Sets the planner's estimate of the cost of "
3939						 "passing each tuple (row) from worker to leader backend."),
3940			NULL,
3941			GUC_EXPLAIN
3942		},
3943		&parallel_tuple_cost,
3944		DEFAULT_PARALLEL_TUPLE_COST, 0, DBL_MAX,
3945		NULL, NULL, NULL
3946	},
3947	{
3948		{"parallel_setup_cost", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3949			gettext_noop("Sets the planner's estimate of the cost of "
3950						 "starting up worker processes for parallel query."),
3951			NULL,
3952			GUC_EXPLAIN
3953		},
3954		&parallel_setup_cost,
3955		DEFAULT_PARALLEL_SETUP_COST, 0, DBL_MAX,
3956		NULL, NULL, NULL
3957	},
3958
3959	{
3960		{"jit_above_cost", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3961			gettext_noop("Perform JIT compilation if query is more expensive."),
3962			gettext_noop("-1 disables JIT compilation."),
3963			GUC_EXPLAIN
3964		},
3965		&jit_above_cost,
3966		100000, -1, DBL_MAX,
3967		NULL, NULL, NULL
3968	},
3969
3970	{
3971		{"jit_optimize_above_cost", PGC_USERSET, QUERY_TUNING_COST,
3972			gettext_noop("Optimize JIT-compiled functions if query is more expensive."),
3973			gettext_noop("-1 disables optimization."),
3974			GUC_EXPLAIN
3975		},

3977		500000, -1, DBL_MAX,
3978		NULL, NULL, NULL
3979	},
3980
3981	{

3985			GUC_EXPLAIN
3986		},

4010		},

4015

4021		},

4031			GUC_EXPLAIN
4032		},
4033		&Geqo_seed,
4034		0.0, 0.0, 1.0,
4035		NULL, NULL, NULL
4036	},
4037
4038	{

4043		},

4045		2.0, 1.0, 1000.0,

4055		2.0, 0.0, 10.0,

4062			NULL,

4070	{

4073			NULL,
4074			GUC_UNIT_MS
4075		},
4076		&VacuumCostDelay,
4077		0, 0, 100,
4078		NULL, NULL, NULL
4079	},
4080
4081	{

4086		},
4087		&autovacuum_vac_cost_delay,
4088		2, -1, 100,
4089		NULL, NULL, NULL
4090	},
4091
4092	{
4093		{"autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor", PGC_SIGHUP, VACUUM_AUTOVACUUM,
4094			gettext_noop("Number of tuple updates or deletes prior to vacuum as a fraction of reltuples."),
4095			NULL
4096		},
4097		&autovacuum_vac_scale,
4098		0.2, 0.0, 100.0,
4099		NULL, NULL, NULL
4100	},
4101
4102	{

4105			NULL
4106		},

4108		0.2, 0.0, 100.0,
4109		NULL, NULL, NULL
